reason: quality_manifest.json is schema-conformant (schema_version 1.0) and self-consistent: fr_ids = [FR-01..FR-05]; nfr_dimension_mapping covers NFR-01..NFR-05 (NFR-06 absent from dimension mapping but present in nfr_traceability — consistent with prior phases); fr_module_traceability maps each FR to its module matching the per-FR Gate-1 fr_id keys; architecture_constraints = [no_circular_dependencies, no_shell_true, atomic_writes_only]; high_risk_modules = [executor, breaker, store]. Per-FR Gate 1 scores (FR-01 96.94 / FR-02 94.22 / FR-03 99.66 / FR-04 99.36 / FR-05 95.92) exactly match 05-verification/BASELINE.md §3-§5 and 05-verification/VERIFICATION_REPORT.md §2.1-§2.5. Gate 2 score 95.65 / Gate 3 100.0 / Gate 4 90.22 all carry quality_complete=true, da_waiver_applied=[architecture], da_waiver_needs_human_review=true — the human-review flag is the framework's documented safety net for hub-and-spoke orchestrator waiver and is appropriately set. Gate 4 score 90.22 is ≥85 release threshold.

gaps.message: Composite drift: .methodology/quality_manifest.json::gate_results.gate4.score (90.22) and .methodology/gate4_result.json::composite_score (90.2222) carry the agent-raw pre-finalize value, while QUALITY_REPORT.md / RELEASE_NOTES.md / FINAL_SIGN_OFF.md cite 91.92 reading from .sessi-work/gate4_result.json (the post-finalize source). harness_cli.py:3201 patches the persistent file on finalize-gate — but the persistent file shows it was never refreshed. Both numbers are ≥85 release threshold (not a blocker), but advance-phase / PhaseAuditor C10 reads the persistent file. Recommend re-running 'python harness_cli.py finalize-gate --gate 4 --phase 6 --project .' to synchronise composite_score across all four artefacts before tag/release.

da_waiver_needs_human_review=true on architecture dimension (quality_manifest.json:149) — this is the framework's documented safety net for hub-and-spoke orchestrator waiver; final sign-off should explicitly record the human reviewer who accepted the da_waiver for taskq/cli.py. RELEASE_NOTES.md §8.1 documents the rationale but does not name a reviewer.
